title: taraxacum subhuelphersianum
intro: Taraxacum subhuelphersianum, a lesser-known variant of the common dandelion, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. Its roots, leaves, and flowers possess medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support liver function. The plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals like potassium and iron, offering numerous health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Taraxacum subhuelphersianum can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
